payload.title: SEPTEMBER 2025 MTA REAL ESTATE FINANCE COMMITTEE AGENDA ITEMS
payload.content_type: Board meeting agenda and staff summaries
payload.description: MTA Real Estate Finance Committee agenda items including administrative actions, transactional actions, and information items for the September 2025 meeting.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.raw_text: SEPTEMBER 2025 MTA REAL ESTATE FINANCE COMMITTEE AGENDA ITEMS

tool_args.label: MTA Construction and Development
tool_args.raw_text: Occupant: MTA Construction and Development (\"C&D\")

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access Project
tool_args.raw_text: PROJECT: Penn Station Access Project (the \"Project\") ... The Project will provide direct rail access to Penn Station-New York and Manhattan's west side from southern Connecticut, Westchester County, and the east Bronx

payload.description: MTA MNR project providing direct rail access to Penn Station-New York from southern Connecticut, Westchester County, and the east Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, with four new Metro-North stations in the East Bronx at Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-op City. Property acquisition phase with September 2025 eminent domain proceedings.

tool_args.label: Nostrand Avenue ADA Project
tool_args.raw_text: AUTHORIZATION TO ACQUIRE PROPERTY INTERESTS BY NEGOTIATED SETTLEMENT OR EMINENT DOMAIN FOR THE NYCT NOSTRAND AVENUE ADA PROJECT IN BROOKLYN, NY

payload.description: NYCT project to construct a new ADA elevator at the Nostrand Avenue subway station, requiring acquisition of 1242 Fulton Street, Brooklyn.

tool_args.label: EDPL Public Hearing for Penn Station Access Project
tool_args.raw_text: On July 24, 2025, the MTA held an EDPL public hearing to describe the public uses, benefits, purposes and location of the work elements covered by the Project

payload.description: MTA held an EDPL public hearing to describe the public uses, benefits, purposes and location of property acquisitions for the Penn Station Access Project

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access property interests needed
tool_args.raw_text: estimated time frames when the property interests would be needed for the Project, currently scheduled to occur in Q1 2026

payload.description: Property interests currently scheduled to be needed for the Penn Station Access Project

tool_args.label: MTA Board authorization for Penn Station Access property acquisition
tool_args.raw_text: On May 28, 2025, the MTA Board authorized the acquisition of required property interests through negotiated agreements or eminent domain

payload.description: MTA Board authorized the acquisition of required property interests through negotiated agreements or eminent domain for the Penn Station Access Project

tool_args.label: Written comment submission deadline for PSA EDPL
tool_args.raw_text: written submissions with comments about the Project and the proposed property acquisitions were received by the MTA prior to the end of the written submission period at the close of business on July 31, 2025, at 5:30 p.m.

payload.description: Deadline for written submissions with comments about the Penn Station Access Project and proposed property acquisitions

tool_args.label: MTA Real Estate Finance Committee Meeting September 2025
tool_args.raw_text: SEPTEMBER 2025 MTA REAL ESTATE FINANCE COMMITTEE AGENDA ITEMS / Finance Committee 09/29/25 X

payload.description: MTA Real Estate Finance Committee meeting to consider agenda items including franchise license policy modification, Penn Station Access eminent domain, Nostrand Avenue ADA property acquisition, lease modifications, and license agreements

tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting September 30, 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Board 09/30/25 X

payload.description: MTA Board meeting to consider approval of real estate items including franchise license modification, Penn Station Access eminent domain, Nostrand Avenue ADA acquisition, lease modifications, and license agreements

tool_args.label: Modification of Franchise License Policy for Telecommunications
tool_args.raw_text: Authorization to modify the Franchise License term from thirty (30) years to forty-nine (49) years

payload.project_name: Modification of Policy to Award Telecommunication and Utility Franchise Licenses
payload.description: Modification to extend the Franchise License term from 30 years to 49 years for the comprehensive installation and maintenance of a broadband network along MTA Agencies rights-of-way

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access travel time savings
tool_args.raw_text: saving riders traveling to Penn Station New York approximately 50 minutes. East Bronx passengers traveling to Connecticut could save up to approximately 75 minutes.

payload.claim_text: The Project will greatly improve transit access for communities in the East Bronx, including low-income and minority communities, saving riders traveling to Penn Station New York approximately 50 minutes. East Bronx passengers traveling to Connecticut could save up to approximately 75 minutes.
payload.subject: Penn Station Access Project

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access noise and vibration impacts
tool_args.raw_text: The Project will increase ambient noise levels by one to four decibels over existing levels and create severe (i.e., significant) noise impacts at 17 buildings (34 dwelling units) and moderate impacts at 270 buildings (765 dwelling units) along the HGL Corridor. ... The Project will create vibration impacts at 40 buildings (84 dwelling units) along the HGL Corridor.

payload.claim_text: The Project will increase ambient noise levels by one to four decibels over existing levels and create severe noise impacts at 17 buildings (34 dwelling units) and moderate impacts at 270 buildings (765 dwelling units) along the HGL Corridor. The Project will create vibration impacts at 40 buildings (84 dwelling units).
payload.subject: Penn Station Access Project

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access construction duration
tool_args.raw_text: Based on conceptual plans and schedule, construction would last approximately five years. There would be approximately 24 to 36 months of consistent station work at each station and up to 36 months of work at each new substation

payload.claim_text: Construction would last approximately five years. There would be approximately 24 to 36 months of consistent station work at each station and up to 36 months of work at each new substation, with shorter durations of work along the HGL Corridor which could occur simultaneously.
payload.subject: Penn Station Access Project

tool_args.label: Franchise license term justification
tool_args.raw_text: it was determined that a forty-nine (49) year term is aligned with the industry standard for similar installation and maintenance of broadband infrastructure and sublicense to telecom vendors

payload.claim_text: A forty-nine (49) year term is aligned with the industry standard for similar installation and maintenance of broadband infrastructure and sublicense to telecom vendors. The term is being increased to 49 years because that is the longest term that may be granted without any risk that the license may be deemed a transfer of an interest in real property.
payload.subject: MTA Franchise License Policy

tool_args.label: Lease Modification and Extension at 29-76 Northern Blvd
tool_args.raw_text: LEASE MODIFICATION AND EXTENSION WITH 29-76 REALTY CO., LLC FOR OFFICE SPACE AT 29-76 NORTHERN BLVD IN QUEENS, NY

payload.project_name: Lease Modification and Extension with 29-76 Realty Co., LLC for Office Space at 29-76 Northern Blvd in Queens, NY
payload.description: Extension and modification of lease for MTA Construction and Development office space, reducing from 34,500 to 17,201 RSF on the 5th floor at 29-76 Northern Boulevard, Long Island City, Queens for 3-year term from November 1, 2025 to October 31, 2028

tool_args.label: Lease with The Saw Pit at Port Chester Station
tool_args.raw_text: LEASE WITH THE SAW PIT AT PORT CHESTER STATION LLC. FOR THE PORT CHESTER STATION BUILDING

payload.project_name: Lease with The Saw Pit at Port Chester Station LLC for Port Chester Station Building
payload.description: 10-year lease for restaurant/brewpub operation at Port Chester Station Building with two 5-year extension options, approximately 5,632 sf building plus 3,600 sf outdoor area and 7 parking spaces

tool_args.label: License with Gateway Foods for Tim Hortons at White Plains Station
tool_args.raw_text: LICENSE WITH GATEWAY FOODS HOLDING LLC FOR THE RETAIL CONCESSION AND STORAGE SPACE AT WHITE PLAINS STATION, WHITE PLAINS, NY

payload.project_name: License with Gateway Foods Holding LLC for Tim Horton's Coffee Concession at White Plains Station
payload.description: 5-year license with 5-year extension option for Tim Horton's coffee concession at White Plains Station, approximately 420 sq. ft. retail concession space and up to 550 sq. ft. storage space

tool_args.label: Easement with AIREF for subsurface utility connections along LIRR Main Line
tool_args.raw_text: EASEMENT WITH AIREF STATION ROAD LOGISTICS CENTER II LLC FOR TWO SUBSURFACE EASEMENTS ALONG LIRR'S MAIN LINE BRANCH NEAR YAPHANK, NY

payload.project_name: Easement with AIREF Station Road Logistics Center II LLC for Two Subsurface Easements along LIRR's Main Line Branch near Yaphank, NY
payload.description: Two permanent subsurface utility easements for sewer force main pipe installation: (1) Bellport Ave crossing in Yaphank (1,350 sq ft); (2) Manor Rd and Munsell Rd crossing in Medford (1,000 sq ft), connecting to Suffolk County Sewer District

tool_args.label: License with Eagle Scaffolding Services at Amityville
tool_args.raw_text: LICENSE WITH EAGLE SCAFFOLDING SERVICES, INC. FOR THE USE PROPERTY AT 67 MILL STREET, AMITYVILLE, NY

payload.project_name: License with Eagle Scaffolding Services, Inc. for Property at 67 Mill Street, Amityville, NY
payload.description: 5-year license for storage of non-hazardous materials and vehicle parking on two parcels totaling approximately 26,625 sq. ft. adjacent to 67 Mill Street, Amityville, Town of Babylon, Suffolk County
